suggestion.description: A credential for user '{trigger.1.name}' can be tested against the open '{trigger.2.name}' service on host {trigger.2.host}.
suggestion.rationale: Credential reuse is a highly effective attack. Credentials found in one context should be tested against all available login services on all discovered hosts.
commands: Attempt to log in to the '{trigger.2.name}' service at {trigger.2.host}:{trigger.2.port} using the username '{trigger.1.name}' and password '{trigger.1.attributes.password}'.
If the credential is a hash ('{trigger.1.attributes.hash}'), attempt Pass-the-Hash attacks for services like SMB and WinRM.

name: Credential Reuse on Discovered Web Login Page

suggestion.description: A credential for user '{trigger.1.name}' can be tested against the discovered web login page '{trigger.2.name}' on host {trigger.2.host}.
suggestion.rationale: Testing known credentials against discovered web application login portals is a high-impact technique for gaining initial access.
commands: Navigate to the login page to verify: http://{trigger.2.host}:{trigger.2.port}{trigger.2.name}
Attempt to log in manually with user '{trigger.1.name}' and password '{trigger.1.attributes.password}'.
